architecture.summary: Grammar prompting augments in-context learning by interleaving a specialized BNF grammar between each demonstration input-output pair. During inference, the LLM first predicts a specialized grammar (a minimal subset of the full DSL grammar sufficient to generate the target output), then generates the output conditioned on that grammar. Constrained decoding uses an Earley parser to enforce grammatical validity: the system speculatively decodes from the LLM, extracts the longest valid prefix using the parser if needed, identifies valid continuations, and uses LLM probabilities to select the next token, iterating until a complete valid program is produced.

biblio.title: Grammar Prompting for Domain-Specific Language Generation with Large Language Models

claims.statement: Grammar prompting achieves 67.1% program accuracy on GeoQuery with unconstrained decoding, compared to 60.7% for standard prompting
Grammar prompting achieves 69.6% program accuracy on GeoQuery with both grammar and program constraints, compared to 60.7% for standard prompting
Grammar prompting achieves 52.4% program accuracy on SMCalFlow with both grammar and program constraints, compared to 46.4% for standard prompting
Grammar prompting achieves 97.9% execution accuracy on GeoQuery in retrieval-based in-context learning, compared to 96.8% for standard prompting
Grammar prompting achieves 90.8% accuracy on GeoQuery NewFunc compositional generalization split (unseen functions), compared to 63.3% for standard prompting

Grammar prompting achieves 98.0% validity, 0.74 diversity, 91.0% retrosynthesis score, and 93.3% membership for Acrylates molecule generation, compared to 87.7% validity, 0.73 diversity, 80.0% retrosynthesis score, and 76.7% membership for standard prompting
Grammar prompting did not yield any improvements for DSLs that were likely to have been frequently encountered during pretraining (e.g., regular expressions, SQL)

Constrained generation based on specialized grammars led to increased API calls (roughly three times more than unconstrained decoding) and was not always beneficial; enforcing constraints in molecule generation resulted in lower diversity, and constraints on object selection in PDDL planning sometimes negatively impacted performance
